# any2md
> Convert any file format to markdown with YAML frontmatter. Local AI on Apple Silicon via MLX — no cloud APIs.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Installation, usage, supported formats, architecture, model pre-download
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Full project structure, module details, architecture, testing, CLI reference

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Auto-detect — just pass any file, URL, or git directory
any2md lecture.mp4                         # audio/video → markdown (Parakeet STT)
any2md podcast.mp3 --diarize               # with speaker diarization
any2md document.pdf                        # PDF → markdown
any2md screenshot.png                      # image → markdown (Qwen VLM OCR)
any2md https://example.com                 # web page → markdown (ReaderLM)
any2md page.html                           # local HTML → markdown
any2md report.docx                         # office doc → markdown
any2md data.csv                            # CSV/TSV → markdown table
any2md config.json                         # JSON/YAML → markdown
any2md app.db                              # SQLite → schema + data
any2md captions.srt                        # subtitles → markdown
any2md notebook.ipynb                      # Jupyter → markdown
any2md message.eml                         # email → markdown
any2md notes.org                           # Org-mode → markdown
any2md paper.tex                           # LaTeX → markdown
any2md command.1                           # man page → markdown
any2md readme.rst                          # RST → markdown
any2md .                                   # git repo → markdown via repomix

# Agent-friendly JSON output (any subcommand)
any2md data.csv --json
any2md config.json --json --fields frontmatter.format,converter
```

## Subcommands

```bash
any2md yt <input>
  # Transcribe audio/video to markdown, SRT, or plain text.
  # Accepts YouTube URLs, YouTube video IDs (11 chars), or local audio/video files.
  # Aliases: audio (MP3/WAV/FLAC/OGG/AAC/M4A), video (MP4/MKV/AVI/MOV/WebM)
  # Requires: mlx-audio[stt], yt-dlp (YouTube), ffmpeg
  --model NAME             # Parakeet alias or HuggingFace ID (default: parakeet-v3)
  --diarize                # Enable Sortformer speaker diarization
  --format md|srt|txt      # Output format (default: md)
  --chunk-duration SECS    # Split long audio into chunks
  --keep-audio             # Keep downloaded audio file

any2md pdf <input>
  # Extract PDF text via pymupdf4llm; optional Qwen3.5 VLM OCR for scanned pages.
  # Auto-falls back to OCR when extraction yields < 50 chars/page.
  # Requires: pymupdf4llm; mlx-vlm for --ocr
  --pages "1-10,15"        # Page range selection
  --ocr                    # Force VLM OCR mode
  --model NAME             # VLM model alias (default: qwen3.5-9b)

any2md img <input>
  # Image OCR via Qwen3.5 vision-language model.
  # Formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WebP, TIFF
  # Requires: mlx-vlm
  --model NAME             # VLM model alias (default: qwen3.5-9b)

any2md web <url>
  # Fetch web URL and convert to markdown via ReaderLM-v2.
  # Requires: mlx-lm, httpx

any2md html <input>
  # Convert local HTML file to markdown via ReaderLM-v2.
  # Supports batch directory conversion.
  # Requires: mlx-lm

any2md doc <input>
  # Convert office documents via markitdown.
  # Formats: DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, EPUB, ODT, RTF
  # Requires: markitdown

any2md rst <input>
  # Convert reStructuredText to markdown.
  # Engine: pypandoc → docutils fallback → pure regex fallback

any2md csv <input>
  # Convert CSV/TSV to markdown tables. Zero dependencies (stdlib only).
  # Auto-detects delimiter.
  --max-rows N             # Row limit (default: 500)
  --max-col-width N        # Column width limit

any2md data <input>
  # Convert JSON/YAML/JSONL to smart markdown. Zero dependencies (stdlib only).
  # Auto-selects table vs bullet vs code block based on structure.

any2md db <input>
  # Export SQLite schema + sample data as markdown. Zero dependencies (stdlib only).
  --max-rows N             # Rows per table (default: 20)
  --max-tables N           # Table limit
  --skip-views             # Exclude views from output

any2md sub <input>
  # Convert subtitles to timestamped markdown via pysubs2.
  # Formats: SRT, VTT, ASS, SSA
  --format md|txt          # Output format

any2md nb <input>
  # Convert Jupyter notebooks to markdown. Zero dependencies (stdlib only).
  # Renders code cells, markdown cells, and cell outputs.
  --no-outputs             # Skip cell output blocks

any2md eml <input>
  # Convert email (.eml or .mbox) to markdown. Zero dependencies (stdlib only).
  # Extracts headers and MIME body.

any2md org <input>
  # Convert Emacs Org-mode to markdown. Pure regex, zero dependencies.

any2md tex <input>
  # Convert LaTeX to markdown. Pure regex, zero dependencies.

any2md man <input>
  # Convert Unix man pages to markdown. Uses mandoc; regex fallback if unavailable.

any2md repo <dir>
  # Convert a git repository to a single markdown file via repomix.
  # Produces YAML frontmatter with file stats. JSON mode passes repomix native JSON.
  # Requires: repomix (npm install -g repomix)
  --style markdown|json    # Output style
  --compress               # Compress output
  --remove-comments        # Strip code comments

any2md speaker             # Manage speaker enrollment catalog (see below)

any2md deps                # Show installed/missing optional dependency status

any2md docs                # Print README documentation to stdout
```

## Global Flags

All subcommands accept:

```bash
--json, -j              # Structured JSON output to stdout (agent-friendly)
--fields FIELDS         # Dot-notation field selection for --json output
                        #   e.g. "frontmatter.rows,content"
--quiet, -q             # Suppress INFO logs (or set ANY2MD_QUIET=1)
--version, -V           # Print version and exit
-o, --output-dir PATH   # Output directory (default: cwd)
-f, --format [md|txt]   # Output format (default: md; yt also supports srt)
-v, --verbose           # Enable DEBUG logging
```

## JSON Output Format

All converters support `--json` for structured stdout:

```json
{
  "frontmatter": { "title": "...", "rows": 42 },
  "content": "## markdown content...",
  "source": "/path/to/file.csv",
  "converter": "csv"
}
```

Use `--fields` to select specific keys and reduce token usage:

```bash
any2md data.csv --json --fields frontmatter.rows,content
```

## Speaker Identification

The `speaker` subcommand manages a local speaker enrollment catalog. When `--diarize` is used during transcription, enrolled speakers are identified by name instead of generic labels like `[SPEAKER_0]`.

### Enrollment workflow

```bash
# 1. Enroll speakers from reference audio clips
any2md speaker add "Alice" --audio alice-sample.mp3
any2md speaker add "Bob" --audio meeting.mp3 --start 15.0 --end 45.0

# Multiple enrollments per speaker improve accuracy
any2md speaker add "Alice" --audio another-clip.wav --start 0 --end 30

# 2. List enrolled speakers
any2md speaker list
any2md speaker list --json

# 3. Transcribe — enrolled speakers are named automatically
any2md yt interview.mp4 --diarize
any2md audio meeting.mp3 --diarize
```

### Catalog management

```bash
any2md speaker remove "Alice"              # Remove speaker and all enrollments
any2md speaker merge "Alice Smith" "Alice" # Merge A into B (A deleted)
any2md speaker stats "Alice"               # Enrollment count, distance stats, meeting count
any2md speaker gallery "Alice"             # All enrollments: source type, date, confidence
```

Speaker catalog stored at `~/.config/any2md/speakers.db` (SQLite). Override with `--db PATH`.

## Key Files

- `src/any2md/cli.py` — unified entry point, auto-detection, subcommand routing
- `src/any2md/common.py` — shared: frontmatter builder, logging, JSON output helpers
- `src/any2md/yt.py` — audio/video/YouTube transcription + speaker diarization
- `src/any2md/pdf.py` — PDF extraction + optional VLM OCR
- `src/any2md/img.py` — image OCR via Qwen3.5 vision-language model
- `src/any2md/web.py` — web URL → markdown via ReaderLM-v2
- `src/any2md/html.py` — local HTML → markdown via ReaderLM-v2
- `src/any2md/doc.py` — office documents via markitdown
- `src/any2md/repo.py` — git repositories via repomix (172-line wrapper)
- `scripts/download_models.py` — pre-download MLX models (--stt, --vlm, --reader, --diarize, --all)
- `tests/` — 808 tests across 19 files (pytest)
